summary: DHH reports being genuinely surprised that Windows has become a viable, even superior alternative to macOS for web development. Through testing a Dell desktop with an i9-14900K and a Framework 13 laptop, he found that WSL combined with VSCode provides seamless Linux integration without performance penalties. While Apple still holds a laptop advantage, desktop Windows machines beat the M3 Max in developer-relevant benchmarks at lower cost. He ultimately didn't switch due to his attachment to TextMate and dislike of Windows font rendering, but argues most web developers would have no such barriers.
key_points: A $2,349 Dell XPS with i9-14900K outperforms a $3,200 M3 Max MacBook in web development benchmarks, including running the HEY test suite in 1m37s vs ~2m16s
WSL + VSCode integration is seamless — files live inside the Linux subsystem, eliminating cross-filesystem performance penalties that plagued earlier attempts
Framework laptops offer a compelling repairability and upgradeability story that makes Apple's forced-upgrade cycle feel wasteful
The tech industry has come full circle: Microsoft was the villain of the 1990s, and now daring to run Windows makes you the odd one out at developer conferences
Apple desperately needs this competition for web developer mindshare, and the Mac finally has a no-apologies-needed alternative
money_quotes: I'm kinda shocked. Windows actually got good for web developers.
WSL lets you run a real Linux distribution natively, so you can use the same package manager that you'll deploy against.
I had somehow bought the premise that an M3 Max was the undisputed king of quick in mainstream computers today. Not so.
It's wild how we've come full circle. Microsoft was the top villain of the late 1990s for everyone I knew in tech, and Apple was the underdog darling. Now it's the other way around.
Switching to OSX in 2001 felt like the rebel move. Now daring to run Windows in 2024 would make you the odd one out in a lot of communities.
Apple desperately needs the competition for the mindshare of web developers.
By burying the hatchet with Linux, and open source in general, you've arrived at an incredibly productive combination of tools for developers like me.
key_insight: Microsoft's embrace of Linux through WSL, combined with competitive x86 desktop hardware, has made Windows a genuinely excellent web development platform that challenges Apple's long-held dominance among developers.
